title: taraxacum officinale subsp. pacheri
intro: Taraxacum officinale subsp. pacheri, commonly known as a subspecies of the dandelion, is a perennial plant with a long history of medicinal use. It is valued for its high content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Taraxacum officinale subsp. pacheri include its potential to support digestive health, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit immune-boosting properties. Taraxacum officinale subsp. pacheri can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, allowing for various forms of medicinal use.
